question_text: A motor receives 1000 J of electrical energy. It converts 700 J into useful mechanical work and 300 J into wasted heat. The motor is then upgraded so that it only wastes 200 J as heat. Calculate the increase in efficiency.
explanation: Original efficiency: (700 ÷ 1000) × 100 = 70%. New efficiency: (800 ÷ 1000) × 100 = 80%. The increase is 80% − 70% = 10%. This is hard because it requires calculating two efficiencies and then finding the difference between them, plus understanding that reducing waste increases useful output.
